pi_items.source: Official Floreant POS documentation confirms '1111' is the default key/PIN. Verified in code: task_utils.sh line 8 (FLOREANT_PIN='1111'), task.json description, and verifier.py line 92.

Official Floreant POS documentation confirms database resides in 'posdb' folder. Verified in code: JDBC URL in setup_task.sh line 45 ('jdbc:derby:/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb'), export_result.sh line 31 (same JDBC URL), and setup_floreant.sh line 125 (backs up '/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server'). Apache Derby uses this path structure for database location.

privileged_info_summary: Floreant POS version 1.4 build 1707b is used in this task. The default manager PIN is 1111 (verified from official Floreant POS documentation as the standard default key). The database is Apache Derby located at /opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb, as confirmed by JDBC connection URLs throughout the task scripts. The task setup programmatically injects an open shift into the database and the agent must close it by accessing the Back Office with PIN 1111. The shift data is synthetic (generated during task setup), but the Floreant POS application itself is the real open-source system downloaded from SourceForge.
